concept.title: Safe Customization Strategy: XSLT-Import Overrides
concept.shortdesc: The recommended customization approach uses XSLT-import extension points so plug-ins override only what they need while remaining upgrade-safe.

section.title: Why this is the best default strategy
section.p: DITA-OT supports XSLT-import extension points, where the plug-in installer adds an xsl:import statement to default code so your override becomes part of the normal build.

ul.li: Upgrade-safe: you import the shipped transform and override specific templates

section.title: Minimal HTML5 XSLT override plugin.xml
section.codeblock: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <?xml-model href="https://www.dita-ot.org/rng/plugin.rnc" type="application/relax-ng-compact-syntax"?> <plugin id="com.yourco.html5.custom" version="1.0.0"> <require plugin="org.dita.html5"/> <feature extension="dita.xsl.html5" file="xsl/html5/override.xsl"/> </plugin>

ul.li: The plugin.xml structure and validation method are described in the official docs.
The XSLT-import mechanism and how it is integrated are described in the XSLT-import extension point docs.
